channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple successfully blocks Match from data in India antitrust case
item.description: Apple has successfully blocked its opponents in India, Tinder-owner Match and a group of startups, from accessing its commercially sensitive information which was part of antitrust findings against the U.S. firm, a confidential order shows.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Moonvalley launches an AI video generator built for moviemaking
item.description: A well-funded AI lab with a deep bench of research talent is releasing a powerful new model that generates high-definition video for the film and advertising industries.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google Updates Chrome Extension Rules Amid PayPal Honey Controversy
item.description: Google updates Chrome extension rules after PayPal's Honey extension was accused of referral jacking, leaving some users questioning Google's motives.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Inside the government study trying to understand the health effects of ultraprocessed foods
item.description: A National Institutes of Health study aims to find out whether ultraprocessed foods lead people to eat more.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge is being tipped to come with a sweet Google Gemini deal
item.description: This could be another reason to invest in the Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge, besides its super-thin design.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: Schools are turning to AI-powered surveillance technology to monitor students on school-issued devices like laptops and tablets.
item.dc:creator: Claire Bryan Of The Seattle Times And Sharon Lurye Of The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ChatGPT wants to write your next novel, and readers and writers alike should be very worried
item.description: Sam Altman shows off a new ChatGPT model that's very good at creative writing, as long as you want robot-written fiction.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: MasOrange se alza con el grueso del contrato de ‘telecos’ del Estado
item.description: MasOrange está de enhorabuena; y es que la operadora que capitanea Meinrad Spenger en su rol de CEO se ha adjudicado el grueso del contrato de servicios de telecomunicaciones de la Administración General del Estado, bautizado como CORA III (Comisión para la Reforma de las Administraciones Públicas,
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft’s plan for genAI profits: Squeeze customers
item.description: Microsoft has ridden its multibillion-dollar investments in generative AI (genAI) to become the world’s second-most valuable company, with a valuation of roughly $3 trillion, depending on the day’s stock price.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: El 70% de las empresas requieren una experiencia de cliente simplificada por parte de sus proveedores telco
item.description: Las expectativas de los clientes empresariales han cambiado. Así lo refrenda la primera edición del estudio anual La perspectiva B2B: las seis principales expectativas de los clientes empresariales de telecomunicaciones firmada por el Instituto de Investigación de Capgemini.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Preparing for the impact of AI in government contracting projects
item.description: The impact of artificial intelligence in government contracting projects is transformative as it creates new avenues to be better and stand out. The post Preparing for the impact of AI in government contracting projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Finally: some truth serum for lying genAI chatbots
item.description: Ever since OpenAI made ChatGPT available to the public in late 2022, the large language model (LLM)-based generative AI (genAI) revolution has advanced at an astonishing pace. Two years and four months ago, we had only ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump buys Tesla to 'make a statement' about Musk backlash
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he was buying a Tesla vehicle as he doubled down on his support of Elon Musk, who has come under mounting scrutiny for leading the Trump administration's government cost initiatives. "I'm going to buy because number one, it's a great product. As good as it gets.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Lummis introduces bill to create bitcoin strategic reserve
item.description: Sen. Cynthia Lummis (R-Wyo.) reintroduced legislation Tuesday to create a bitcoin strategic reserve, seeking to codify President Trump’s executive order last week establishing a reserve.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: South Dakota pushes labels for political deepfake videos ahead of elections
item.description: South Dakota lawmakers have approved legislation that would impose criminal and civil penalties on those who try to use unlabeled deepfakes to influence voters in the state, with the law now awaiting signature from Gov. Larry Rhoden.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: DOGE deploys AI chatbot at GSA amid workforce reductions
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), backed by Elon Musk, has expanded the deployment of its custom AI-powered chatbot, GSAi, to approximately 1,500 government workers at the General Services Administration.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get more than $25M
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Weaviate adds agents to its tech stack to ease gen AI app development
item.description: Vector database provider Weaviate has added three new agents to its development stack to facilitate the development of generative AI-based applications. These agents are modular agentic workflows that use large language models (LLMs) and prompts to interact dynamically with data in Weaviate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IBM wins UK lawsuit against LzLabs for mainframe intellectual property theft
item.description: When it comes to protecting its mainframe technology, IBM wields a pretty big sword. This week, its defense triumphed as IBM won a judgement against LzLabs for violating Big Blue’s intellectual property rights.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mark Kelly fires back at Musk over 'traitor' accusation
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) hit back at tech billionaire Elon Musk after he called Kelly a "traitor" for visiting Ukraine and demanding the United States stand with Ukrainians in their ongoing war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft reportedly struggling to build its own reasoning models to rival OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft may be “all in” with AI, but its efforts to produce homegrown products to feed its ambitions have so far been lagging and its reliance on OpenAI prevails. However, recent events may augur change.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lenovo introduces compact, liquid-cooled AI edge server
item.description: Lenovo has announced the ThinkEdge SE100, an entry-level AI inferencing server that’s designed to make edge AI affordable for enterprises as well as small and medium-sized businesses. AI systems are not normally small and compact; they’re big, decked out servers with lots of memory, GPUs, and CPUs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: "We have a lot of interest in TikTok. China is going to play a role, so hopefully China will approve of the deal," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Seven important trends in the server sphere
item.description: The pace of change around server technology is advancing considerably, driven by hyperscalers but spilling over into the on-premises world as well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KRON4
